searchLedger.hypothesis: FTS early return applies filters before LIMIT

searchActions.result: bm25Search delegates to ftsSearch at hybrid-search.ts:407-424. ftsSearch delegates to fts5Bm25Search at hybrid-search.ts:561-565. sqlite-fts.ts:179-200 applies spec-folder and deprecated-tier filters in WHERE before ORDER BY and LIMIT.

searchLedger.rationale: The early FTS branch does not retain the old limit-before-filter bug for positive limits.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: SQL filter order is WHERE filters, then ORDER BY, then LIMIT.

searchLedger.hypothesis: In-memory BM25 path filters before final slice

searchActions.result: The patched path filters deprecated tier and specFolder at hybrid-search.ts:480-489, then applies .slice(0, limit) at hybrid-search.ts:490 and maps source metadata at hybrid-search.ts:491-494.

searchLedger.rationale: For positive limits, filtered survivors are capped only after scope/deprecated filtering.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: No alternate return path in the in-memory branch slices before the metadata filter.

searchLedger.hypothesis: documentCount is sufficient to fetch all query-matching BM25 candidates

searchActions.result: getStats returns documentCount at bm25-index.ts:655-660. Legacy search sorts and slices to the passed limit at bm25-index.ts:650-652; packed search does the same at bm25-index.ts:1326-1330.

searchLedger.rationale: Passing documentCount fetches every scored matching document up to corpus size, so survivors are not missed because the BM25 search cap itself is too small.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: The remaining over-fetch risk is the metadata lookup batch size, reported separately.

searchLedger.hypothesis: No-db/no-specFolder path preserves old candidate limit

searchActions.result: candidateLimit uses documentCount only when specFolder || db is truthy; otherwise it remains limit at hybrid-search.ts:427-430.

searchLedger.rationale: For positive limits with neither db nor specFolder, bm25Search still asks index.search for only limit candidates and applies no metadata filter.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Behavior parity is preserved for the no-db/no-scope path.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Empty matches and specFolder-without-db fail closed

searchActions.result: Metadata lookup only runs when results.length > 0 at hybrid-search.ts:443. If specFolder is present and db is unavailable, bm25Search returns [] at hybrid-search.ts:444-448. Empty result arrays fall through the filter/slice/map chain at hybrid-search.ts:480-494 as [].

searchLedger.rationale: documentCount===0, no query matches, limit greater than survivors, and db-null scoped queries do not leak unscoped candidates.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: These edge paths return empty or all available survivors as expected for positive limits.

title: Corpus-sized over-fetch can overflow the metadata lookup and return no BM25 results
evidence: candidateLimit becomes index.getStats().documentCount when specFolder or db is present, then index.search(query, candidateLimit) is used at hybrid-search.ts:427-433. The code then builds one SQL placeholder per returned candidate at hybrid-search.ts:452-456 and returns [] on any lookup error at hybrid-search.ts:464-467. bm25-index.ts:633-652 and bm25-index.ts:1297-1330 confirm index.search caps at the provided argument, so documentCount can produce one candidate per matching document.
rationale: For high-recall queries in a corpus with more matching docs than SQLite's bind-parameter limit, the metadata SELECT ... IN (...) can fail and the catch path returns an empty array. That means scoped and db-present unscoped BM25 searches can still under-return despite valid in-scope, non-deprecated survivors.

title: Negative direct limits have inconsistent non-capping behavior
evidence: bm25Search takes limit without local normalization at hybrid-search.ts:404. The FTS branch passes it through at hybrid-search.ts:408-410 to sqlite-fts.ts:168 and sqlite-fts.ts:185-200, while the in-memory path uses .slice(0, limit) at hybrid-search.ts:490.
rationale: limit=0 returns empty, but negative limits can behave as an unbounded SQL LIMIT in the FTS path and as a negative JS slice in the in-memory path. The primary memory_search handler clamps limits to positive values, so this is limited to direct/internal helper callers.
